reasoning: The Lean theorem faithfully encodes the informal claim. The hypothesis `card G = 105` (with `open Fintype`) is `Fintype.card G = 105`, which matches |G|=105. The conclusion `(∃ P : Sylow 5 G, P.Normal) ∧ (∃ P : Sylow 7 G, P.Normal)` exactly says "G has a normal Sylow 5-subgroup and a normal Sylow 7-subgroup", since `P.Normal` for `P : Sylow p G` means the underlying subgroup is normal in G. The `[Fintype G]` instance is a reasonable encoding of "finite group" implied by |G|=105. No extra hypotheses or conclusions are introduced, and the quantifier structure matches the informal statement.
